lesson.title: Personal Tax Obligations (SARS)
lesson.explanation: SA residents taxed on worldwide income. 2025/26 brackets: R0-R237,100 at 18%, R237,101-R370,500 at 26%, R370,501-R512,800 at 31%, up to 45% above R1,817,000. Tax threshold: under 65 = R95,750 (no tax if below). File via SARS eFiling or MobiApp by October (non-provisional) or January (provisional). Deductions: retirement fund contributions (27.5% of remuneration, max R350,000), medical tax credits (R364/month for main member). Provisional tax: if income not fully taxed via PAYE (freelancers, rental income). Penalties: 10% for late filing + interest on late payment.

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Personal Tax Obligations (SARS)
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
